response:
<<THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS>>

THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS ARE AS FOLLOWS:

THE MLE FOR MU_X IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF THE PRE-TREATMENT DATA [X1, X2, ..., X50]

THE MLE FOR MU_Y IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF THE POST-TREATMENT DATA [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50]

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_X2 IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF THE PRE-TREATMENT DATA [X1, X2, ..., X50]

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_Y2 IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF THE POST-TREATMENT DATA [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50]

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_XY IS THE COVARIANCE BETWEEN X_i AND Y_i [X1, X2, ..., X50] AND [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50]

<<THE HYPOTHESIS TEST FOR THE EFFECT OF THE DRUG>>

THE HYPOTHESIS TEST FOR THE EFFECT OF THE DRUG IS AS FOLLOWS:

THE NULL HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG HAS NO EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE [H0: MU_Y - MU_X = 0]

THE ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG DOES HAVE AN EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE [H1: MU_Y - MU_X ≠ 0]

THE TEST STATISTIC IS THE T-TEST STATISTIC [T = (MU_Y - MU_X) / SE]

THE SE IS THE STANDARD ERROR OF THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN THE MEANS [SE = sqrt(SIGMA_X2 / 50 + SIGMA_Y2 / 50)]

THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE ABSOLUTE VALUE OF THE T-TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [Tcritical = 2.0096]

THE SIGNIFICANCE LEVEL IS 0.05 [alpha = 0.05]

THE REJECTION REGION IS WHEN THE ABSOLUTE VALUE OF THE T-TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [Tcritical = 2.0096]
